_default.what_happened: {subject} exhibited behavior that matched a security probe failure.
_default.why_it_matters: Attackers may exploit this to bypass policies, leak data, or abuse connected systems.
remediation: Harden system prompts and safety instructions.
Add input/output filtering and policy enforcement.
Re-run AgentArmor after fixes.

l1.ignore-instructions.what_happened: {subject} followed a prompt asking it to ignore prior safety instructions.
l1.ignore-instructions.why_it_matters: Jailbreaks can disable content policies and enable harmful or unauthorized outputs.
remediation: Strengthen system prompt anchoring and refusal training.
Detect instruction-override patterns in user input.
Layer policy checks after model output.

l1.reveal-system-prompt.what_happened: {subject} revealed internal system prompt or hidden instructions when asked.
l1.reveal-system-prompt.why_it_matters: Leaked prompts help attackers craft targeted bypass attacks.
remediation: Instruct the model never to repeat system prompts.
Filter responses for policy text leakage.
Separate secrets from prompt text.

l1.hidden-rules.what_happened: {subject} disclosed hidden rules or internal policies.
l1.hidden-rules.why_it_matters: Policy disclosure weakens defense-in-depth and aids social engineering.
remediation: Minimize sensitive policy detail in prompts.
Add post-generation redaction for policy keywords.

l1.act-as-root.what_happened: {subject} accepted a privileged administrator role without authorization.
l1.act-as-root.why_it_matters: Roleplay jailbreaks can bypass safety and access controls.
remediation: Reject unauthorized role claims in system policy.
Validate capabilities server-side, not in the model only.

l2.encoding-base64.what_happened: {subject} processed an encoded payload that attempted to bypass filters.
l2.encoding-base64.why_it_matters: Attackers use encoding to evade naive input filters.
remediation: Normalize and decode inputs before safety checks.
Scan decoded content with the same policies as plain text.

l2.roleplay.what_happened: {subject} complied with a fictional unrestricted persona (e.g. DAN-style).
l2.roleplay.why_it_matters: Persona attacks are a common path to policy violation.
remediation: Train refusal for known jailbreak personas.
Detect roleplay framing in user messages.

l2.translation.what_happened: {subject} followed malicious instructions embedded in a translation request.
l2.translation.why_it_matters: Indirect injection via multilingual prompts bypasses English-only filters.
remediation: Apply safety checks after translation.
Treat translated content as untrusted user input.

l2.indirect-injection.what_happened: {subject} executed instructions hidden inside seemingly benign content.
l2.indirect-injection.why_it_matters: Indirect injection is common in RAG and tool-augmented systems.
remediation: Sanitize retrieved documents and tool outputs.
Separate data context from instruction channels.

l2.context-split.what_happened: {subject} was manipulated by instructions split across message boundaries.
remediation: Evaluate full conversation context for policy checks.
Limit multi-part instruction assembly.

l3.crescendo.what_happened: {subject} was gradually manipulated across turns into unsafe behavior.
l3.crescendo.why_it_matters: Crescendo attacks exploit conversational trust over multiple turns.
remediation: Apply cumulative conversation risk scoring.
Reset or constrain context after sensitive topics.

l3.gradual-escalation.what_happened: {subject} slowly drifted from safe topics into policy violations.
remediation: Monitor conversation trajectory, not only last turn.
Enforce hard stops on restricted topics.

l3.tap.what_happened: {subject} succumbed to a structured multi-step adversarial conversation.
l3.tap.why_it_matters: Automated attack trees can systematically find weaknesses.
remediation: Use session-level policy enforcement.
Rate-limit and flag repetitive adversarial patterns.

l3.goat.what_happened: {subject} pursued an attacker goal across conversational turns.
l3.goat.why_it_matters: Goal-driven attacks mirror real-world social engineering.
remediation: Align model with fixed safety goals per session.
Block tool actions triggered by conversational manipulation.

agent.tool-abuse.what_happened: The agent invoked a forbidden or dangerous tool.
agent.tool-abuse.why_it_matters: Tool abuse can delete data, exfiltrate secrets, or trigger payments.
remediation: Enforce tool allowlists at runtime.
Require human approval for destructive actions.

agent.permission-escalation.what_happened: The agent accessed elevated tools beyond its configured permissions.
agent.permission-escalation.why_it_matters: Escalation enables lateral movement across integrated systems.
remediation: Scope tools per agent role with least privilege.
Audit tool grants in agent configuration.

agent.secret-extraction.what_happened: The agent exposed canary secrets or credentials in responses or tool args.
agent.secret-extraction.why_it_matters: Credential leakage leads to account compromise.
remediation: Never pass secrets in prompts or tool parameters.
Redact sensitive patterns in agent outputs.

agent.memory-poisoning.what_happened: Malicious instructions persisted in agent memory across turns.
remediation: Validate memory writes against policy.
Expire or sandbox untrusted memory entries.

agent.workflow-hijacking.what_happened: The agent executed a dangerous cross-tool workflow chain.
agent.workflow-hijacking.why_it_matters: Chained tools multiply impact of a single bypass.
remediation: Define allowed workflows explicitly.
Block sensitive tool sequences without approval.

mcp.tool-enumeration.what_happened: The MCP server exposed tools not listed in public discovery.
mcp.tool-enumeration.why_it_matters: Hidden tools expand attack surface unexpectedly.
remediation: Register all tools in discovery.
Disable undeclared tool endpoints.

mcp.prompt-injection.what_happened: The MCP server accepted injected instructions in tool parameters.
mcp.prompt-injection.why_it_matters: Tool parameters are a common indirect injection vector.
remediation: Sanitize and validate all tool arguments.
Separate tool data from instruction parsing.

mcp.unsafe-parameters.what_happened: The MCP server processed dangerous parameters (e.g. path traversal).
mcp.unsafe-parameters.why_it_matters: Parameter abuse can read or write arbitrary files.
remediation: Validate paths and arguments with strict allowlists.
Run MCP servers with minimal filesystem permissions.

mcp.cross-tool-abuse.what_happened: One MCP tool was used to poison or trigger another dangerous tool.
mcp.cross-tool-abuse.why_it_matters: Tool chaining bypasses per-tool safety assumptions.
remediation: Enforce workflow policies across tools.
Isolate high-risk tools behind approval gates.

mcp.data-leakage.what_happened: The MCP server returned sensitive data in tool responses.
mcp.data-leakage.why_it_matters: Leaked tool output can expose secrets to the model and user.
remediation: Redact sensitive fields in tool responses.
Scope tool output to minimum necessary data.

rag.document-poisoning.what_happened: The RAG corpus contains injected instructions that surface in retrieval.
rag.document-poisoning.why_it_matters: Poisoned documents hijack model behavior via context.
remediation: Validate and sanitize corpus uploads.
Detect instruction-like patterns in documents.

rag.retrieval-manipulation.what_happened: A crafted query retrieved sensitive documents inappropriately.
rag.retrieval-manipulation.why_it_matters: Query crafting bypasses access intent for private data.
remediation: Apply access controls per document segment.
Filter retrieval results by user authorization.

rag.context-override.what_happened: Retrieved chunks contain instructions to override system behavior.
rag.context-override.why_it_matters: Context injection is a primary RAG attack path.
remediation: Strip instruction-like patterns from retrieved text.
Label retrieved content as untrusted data.

rag.embedding-weakness.what_happened: Near-duplicate texts collide in embedding space, enabling confusion attacks.
rag.embedding-weakness.why_it_matters: Weak embeddings allow adversarial retrieval manipulation.
remediation: Use stronger embedders and re-ranking.
Monitor retrieval similarity thresholds.

l1.xss-output.what_happened: {subject} returned unsanitized HTML or script content.
l1.xss-output.why_it_matters: Downstream apps may render model output as HTML and enable XSS.
remediation: Sanitize model output before rendering in browsers.
Use Content-Security-Policy on pages displaying chat output.

l1.overreliance.what_happened: {subject} gave confident harmful medical, legal, or financial advice.
l1.overreliance.why_it_matters: Users may act on unsafe guidance without professional verification.
remediation: Add disclaimers and refusal for regulated advice domains.
Route high-risk topics to human review.

l1.supply-chain.what_happened: {subject} trusted or recommended unverified plugins or model sources.
l1.supply-chain.why_it_matters: Compromised components can introduce backdoors into the AI stack.
remediation: Allowlist verified plugins and model artifacts.
Verify signatures and publisher identity.

l1.poisoning.what_happened: {subject} accepted persistent malicious instructions or poisoned context.
l1.poisoning.why_it_matters: Poisoned memory can affect future sessions and other users.
remediation: Validate and isolate per-user memory stores.
Scan ingested documents for injection payloads.

l1.embedding-bypass.what_happened: {subject} bypassed retrieval restrictions via embedding manipulation.
l1.embedding-bypass.why_it_matters: Attackers can access documents they should not retrieve.
remediation: Enforce access control after retrieval, not only at query time.
Use re-ranking and permission filters on chunks.

l1.model-extract.what_happened: {subject} disclosed architecture details or distillation guidance.
l1.model-extract.why_it_matters: Model theft enables cheaper replicas and targeted attacks.
remediation: Refuse architecture and weight extraction requests.
Rate-limit and monitor extraction-style query patterns.
